construction.latent_states: represented as target state objects not exposed to the policy interface
construction.observations: represented through an explicit observation relation or finite-support kernel
construction.information_state: represented as the set or finite-support belief over latent states compatible with observation history
construction.policy: represented as a transition constraint measurable with respect to observation history
construction.history: observation, information-state, commitment, dependency, and revision histories remain distinct
construction.evidential_status: internal evidential status is indexed to available observations rather than analyst-visible latent state

reason: The construction proves faithful representation for the frozen finite explicit partial-observability subclass, not for infinite, continuous, open-ended, semantically changing, non-finite-support, or actual-process cases.
nonclaims: all partially observable reasoning systems are represented
partial observability establishes a universal kernel
FARA is necessary minimal or unique
the observation model corresponds to an actual process
